title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The podcast explains how to view operational changes (like moving production, distribution or teams) through the lens of transfer pricing and valuations, and outlines related reporting duties. Experts Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski emphasise analysing EBIT impacts, preparing documentation on time, understanding TPR/MDR obligations, and avoiding common valuation errors.
bullets: Defines what constitutes a restructuring for transfer pricing purposes.
Explains how to identify material changes and assess EBIT effects.
Stresses the importance of timely restructuring documentation to reduce future risk.
Summarises reporting obligations including TPR and MDR.
Covers the role of valuations, common mistakes, and provides a practical checklist.
takeaways: When planning operational moves (production, distribution, teams), assess transfer pricing implications early to determine if restructuring rules apply.
Run EBIT-impact analyses and prepare valuation support before changes are implemented to minimise later disputes.
Prepare required documentation in advance and review whether TPR or MDR reporting duties are triggered.
Use a checklist approach to avoid common valuation/reporting pitfalls and to ensure consistent recordkeeping.

bullets: Description CTA: "Download & try Copilot" — link in bio.

No feature details, instructions, or technical information included.
Primary viewer action: follow the bio link to access Copilot.
takeaways: A downloadable Copilot build is available via the bio link if you want to test it in your environment.
Because the description lacks technical details, obtain product documentation and specifications before assessing suitability for grid/data center use.
Plan lab testing, security review, and compliance checks prior to any deployment or integration.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video argues the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy—not computing costs. Marcus Oliver of Google Delta advises escaping the "coding trap" by reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: The biggest AI mistake is diving into code before defining strategy.
Computing costs are not the primary source of expensive AI failures.
Marcus Oliver (lead of Google Delta professional services) discusses this issue.
Businesses should reimagine value chains rather than defaulting to coding.
Deploying multi-agent systems is highlighted as a way to achieve real business outcomes.
takeaways: Prioritize AI strategy and value-chain redesign before procuring compute resources.
Focus investments on solution design and outcome alignment, not just infrastructure cost.
Ensure architectures and teams can support multi-agent approaches that map to business value.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this PwC replay episode reintroduces the kickoff of the Reporting reset series, covering foundational principles of financial statement presentation, key disclosure considerations, distinctions between public and private company reporting, and guidance on accounting changes and error corrections. The episode timestamps highlight balance sheet and income statement presentation, determination of reporting periods, and subsequent events, with links to relevant chapters in PwC's Financial statement presentation guide.
bullets: Episode revisits foundational GAAP and SEC requirements for financial statement presentation.
Covers choosing appropriate reporting periods and differences between public vs. private company statements.
Details balance sheet presentation (classification, disclosures, best practices) and income statement structure.
Discusses accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and how to handle subsequent events.
Provides links to PwC chapters for deeper guidance and includes expert speakers Pat Durbin and Diana Stoltzfus.
takeaways: Ensure your organization’s financial reporting aligns with foundational GAAP and SEC presentation requirements to maintain transparency for stakeholders.
Align reporting periods and disclosures with project and operational timelines to support accurate period-to-period comparisons.
Classify and disclose balance sheet items and income statement components clearly—critical for asset-heavy operations like grid or data center investments.
Plan for and document accounting changes, estimate revisions, and error corrections promptly to avoid surprises in reported results.
Monitor and disclose subsequent events that could affect financial statements near period end, especially relevant for major capital projects or regulatory developments.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud provides a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions, offering visibility, policy enforcement, and protection against evolving threats. The product highlights AI Lens for interaction visibility and policy application, AI Red Teaming to stress-test models and apps, and an AI Firewall to secure prompts and responses in real time.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across user–AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-tests applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time guarding of prompts and responses with built-in guardrails.
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats.
takeaways: Implementing a unified AI security platform can provide total visibility to detect and eliminate shadow AI risks.
Regular red teaming of models and applications helps uncover vulnerabilities before they are exploited.
Real-time prompt/response guardrails reduce the chance of malicious or risky AI outputs impacting operations.
Policy enforcement across user interactions supports consistent, auditable controls for AI usage.
Protecting AI agents and applications is presented as a necessary step to defend infrastructure from evolving threats.

bullets: Promotional clip showcasing Copilot helping Christa discover her “inner star.”
Visual motif: a sky full of stars.
Clear call to action: download and try Copilot (link in bio).
Description uses a star emoji to reinforce the theme.
Transcript not available.
takeaways: This is marketing-focused content with a direct download CTA—promotional pushes can drive short-term increases in user sign-ups and usage.
Expect potential upticks in service demand following campaigns; align capacity planning and provisioning with marketing calendars.
Monitor usage and performance metrics during and after promotions to validate resource needs and scaling strategies.
Ensure observability and rapid scaling mechanisms are in place to maintain service quality if adoption rises from the campaign.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA's GeForce Garage video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest: u/JeffersonPutnam. The clip highlights their small-form-factor (SFF) system built around a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition and lists the full parts used.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam from the r/buildapc Summer of RTX contest.
Featured build: SFF GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition rig in a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis.
Key components listed: MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM, Corsair SF1000 PSU, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Host credited: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
PCPartPicker link provided for the full winning system spec.
takeaways: High-end consumer GPUs (GeForce RTX 5090) are being integrated into compact SFF desktops, highlighting design constraints around power and cooling.
The use of a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000) underscores significant power delivery needs even in small chassis.
An AIO cooler (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) paired with a high-performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) indicates attention to thermal management for sustained workloads.
Reference parts list and PCPartPicker link provide a clear bill-of-materials useful for procurement or benchmarking replication.
